meta.title: Pipeline Test 2 — Holding a Boundary
meta.subtitle: Second sample used to verify server delivery.
meta.description: A throwaway 3-card sample published to confirm a second item flows through the content pipeline. Safe to delete.
meta.author: AI

cards.title: State It Once, Flat
cards.remember: Say the line once. Repeat it, don't defend it.
cards.hook: Explaining a boundary invites negotiation of it.
cards.theySee: Someone calm and immovable — no argument to push against.
cards.script: Say it plainly: 'I'm not able to do that.' If pressed, repeat the same sentence, same tone — don't add reasons.
cards.counter: When someone gives you a flat 'no,' don't mine for a reason to attack — accept it or make a different request.

cards.title: Name the Push
cards.remember: Say what they're doing out loud. Pressure hates daylight.
cards.hook: Naming a tactic disarms it mid-swing.
cards.theySee: Someone who notices the squeeze and says so, without heat.
cards.script: Label it neutrally: 'It feels like there's some pressure to decide right now.' Then pause and let them respond.
cards.counter: If someone labels your move, don't deny it — acknowledge and slow down rather than pushing harder.

cards.title: Exit Is a Sentence
cards.remember: A boundary with no exit is a suggestion.
cards.hook: The line only holds if leaving is on the table.
cards.theySee: Someone willing to end the conversation — so the line is real.
cards.script: Pre-write your exit: 'If this keeps going, I'm going to step away.' Then actually do it if the line is crossed.
cards.counter: When someone signals they'll walk, take it seriously — escalating only confirms their reason to leave.
